subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 13
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which animal is known as the 'ship of the desert'?
questions.explanation: Camels are called the 'ship of the desert' because they can travel long distances in the desert without water.
questions.hint: They have one or two humps on their backs.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their adaptations.

questions.q: What is the currency used in the United States?
questions.explanation: The US dollar is the official currency of the United States.
questions.hint: It comes in coins and bills.

questions.q: Which of these is a primary color?
questions.explanation: The three primary colors are red, blue, and yellow.
questions.hint: It is the color of the clear sky.

questions.q: How many days are there in a week?
questions.explanation: There are seven days in a week: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, and Sunday.

questions.q: Which organ in the human body helps you breathe?
questions.explanation: Lungs take in oxygen when you breathe in and release carbon dioxide when you breathe out.
questions.hint: They are located in your chest.

questions.q: Which of these is a renewable resource?
questions.explanation: Sunlight is renewable because it will keep shining for billions of years.
questions.hint: It is free energy from the sky.

questions.q: What is the freezing point of water in degrees Fahrenheit?
questions.hint: It is the temperature at which snow starts to form.

questions.q: Who is the leader of a city?
questions.explanation: A mayor is the head of the local government of a city or town.

questions.q: Which animal is the only mammal capable of true flight?
questions.explanation: Bats are the only mammals that can actually fly, rather than just gliding.
questions.hint: They are nocturnal and hang upside down.

questions.q: What is the chemical symbol for water?
questions.explanation: Water is made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, hence H2O.
questions.hint: It is a very famous scientific formula.
